paragraphs.text:
SRD-46 reports only **two Cu(II)–glycine stability species** for this system: **ML** and **ML₂**. I did **not** find protonated, hydroxo, or polynuclear Cu(II)–glycine species for `metal_41` + `ligand_5760` in the returned SRD-46 network/stability records.

| Species | Equilibrium | Constant reported |
|---|---|---|
| ML | Cu²⁺ + L ⇌ CuL | logK (stepwise / overall for 1:1) |
| ML₂ | Cu²⁺ + 2L ⇌ CuL₂ | logβ₂ (overall for 1:2) |

Here **L** is deprotonated glycine, consistent with the SRD-46 equations shown as `[L]`.

Standard relation: overall β2 = K1·K2, so K2 = β2/K1. Database lists β1(=K1) and β2 but not this formula explicitly.

paragraphs.text:
## Thermodynamic quantities also reported

| Species | Quantity | Value | T (°C) | I (M) |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| ML | ΔH | -26.8 | 10 | 0 |
| ML | ΔH | -25.1 | 25 | 0 |
| ML | ΔH | -25.1 | 25 | 0.1 |
| ML | ΔH | -23.4 | 40 | 0 |
| ML | ΔS | 79.5 | 25 | 0 |
| ML | ΔS | 72.4 | 25 | 0.1 |
| ML₂ | ΔH | -56.5 | 10 | 0 |
| ML₂ | ΔH | -54.4 | 25 | 0 |
| ML₂ | ΔH | -54.4 | 25 | 0.1 |
| ML₂ | ΔH | -52.3 | 40 | 0 |
| ML₂ | ΔS | 118 | 25 | 0 |
| ML₂ | ΔS | 106.7 | 25 | 0.1 |

A quick chemistry takeaway: Cu(II) binds glycine strongly, with **logK₁ around 8–9** and **logβ₂ around 15–16**, and the second ligand binds less strongly than the first, as expected from stepwise complexation. The modest decrease at higher temperature and some dependence on ionic strength are also consistent with normal metal–amino acid complexation behavior.

**Source:** NIST SRD-46, Cu²⁺ (`metal_41`) + aminoacetic acid/glycine (`ligand_5760`) stability and network records.
